methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $8.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Wayve, a Series D AI & Software company founded by Amar Shah and Alex Kendall, is evaluated against a peer universe comprising one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private-stage peers including Figure AI, Skild AI, Crusoe Energy, and Ayar Labs.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression methodology, applied to the company's last post-money valuation of $8.6B, implies a Series D-to-subsequent-stage step-up of 1.5x, yielding $12.9B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $12.0B, with a range spanning $3.8B (Ayar Labs, Series E) to $39.0B (Figure AI, Series C). The wide dispersion reflects material stage and development heterogeneity within the frontier-AI cohort.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $10.2B (low) to $14.8B (high), with a median of $12.4B. This positioning sits modestly above the target's current $8.6B post-money, consistent with expected progression within Series D. Key uncertainties include the extreme valuation variance in peer comparables—a 10x spread—and the limited depth of directly comparable late-stage AI infrastructure companies. Capital efficiency relative to raised capital ($2.8B to date) and pathway to sustainable unit economics remain material but data-constrained inputs to terminal value estimation.
